mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in durable and residential demand, which compounds corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested exclusively during recession or high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real discount rates and prompts expectations of central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months, or inflation fails to correlate with subsequent rate hike expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, providing a stable earnings floor that supports positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDP growth combined with declining real sales of final goods signals a broad demand contraction, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined weak-demand signal fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent series effects in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks or demand destruction, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances following a sharp industrial production decline, or the signal loses significance during supply-constrained regimes.
